While the extremely pricey menu options were surprisingly good, the service in the whole restaurant was awful! Our server came to our table twice the entire night. Once to seat us, and once after we waited a very long time to get our check. He never came to check on us or ask how our food was even once! One table next to us even walked out before getting their food, or maybe they just never received it. A couple sat at the hostess stand for a good 10 mins before anyone greeted them as well. I'm surprised they didn't leave! It's too bad that the staff was so un attentive, because the food was actually pretty good! We will probably not be returning here:/"}, {'review_stars': 4.0, 'review_date': '2014-04-27 01:25:26', 'review_text': "I frequent this location often on the weekends as a hang-out location with my friends. Over the many time I have been here, I have had a consistent experience with the food, the environment, as well as the service. 

When you open the menu, you will be quite surprised at the wide variety of choices available. Dishes range from crab-cakes to artichoke dips to filet mignons. Personally, I have tried the steaks many times at Holdrens, as well as a few of their select appetizers. The food is good for the price, typically around $20-$30 per individual (Combination of entree and appetizer). To me, having frequented many steakhouses in the United States such as Mastro's and Strip House, I would have to say that the quality and presentation of the slab of meat is on par with my expectations. It might be a little bit on the high side in terms of the food, but what can you expect, it is Santa Barbara. I expect the price to be more catered to the location when looking at the prices. Overall, I was happy with my food because it was delicious and I left the restaurant a happy customer. 

Many people on Yelp have been complaining about the poor service and lack of attention they received during their experience. During my few times at Holdren's, I have yet to encounter any problems. The service is excellent and attentive, and my table was always served and cared for properly. 

Overall, my experience at Holdren's was pleasant. I understand that it is located near Santa Barbara, which is prime real-estate. I expect many restaurants to institute a premium in their prices because of this. As a happy customer, I will continue to frequent this location with my friends when I do have the chance."}, {'review_stars': 1.0, 'review_date': '2014-04-16 20:31:00', 'review_text': "My family went there for my brother's birthday. The wait staff was polite and courteous but the food was terrible. The steaks were burnt and the fish was undercooked. My mom and I split the bill and they ended up charging me for the entire bill!  I spoke with the manager a few days later and she said she would refund the portion I didn't owe, and instead CHARGED me again. Thank god I had enough money in my account to cover the charges. My bill was $86 and instead I got charged twice for a total of $411.08. Ridiculous, they lost me and my family's business forever."}]}

{'name': 'Mala Town', 'address': '6555 Pardall Rd', 'city': 'Goleta', 'state': 'CA', 'categories': 'Chinese, Restaurants', 'hours': {'Monday': '17:0-21:0', 'Tuesday': '17:0-21:0', 'Wednesday': '11:0-15:0', 'Thursday': '17:0-21:0', 'Friday': '11:0-22:0', 'Saturday': '12:0-22:0', 'Sunday': '12:0-22:0'}, 'attributes': {'BusinessParking': {'garage': False, 'street': False, 'validated': False, 'lot': False, 'valet': False}, 'RestaurantsReservations': None, 'OutdoorSeating': True, 'WiFi': None, 'RestaurantsTakeOut': None, 'RestaurantsGoodForGroups': None, 'Music': None, 'Ambience': {'touristy': False, 'hipster': False, 'romantic': False, 'divey': False, 'intimate': False, 'trendy': False, 'upscale': False, 'classy': False, 'casual': False}}, 'business_stars': 4.0, 'review_info': [{'review_stars': 4.0, 'review_date': '2019-11-22 07:59:18', 'review_text': 'A friend and I ate lunch at Mala Town today. We arrived at noon and were the only ones there. The large and lush patio was great since the weather was nice, and my bowl of spicy soup noodles hit the spot. 

I used to live in Hong Kong and have traveled in Asia quite a bit. While I can't quite call what I ate "traditional" in some respects, it was delicious. For around $11.80, I got a big bowl of tasty mala soup (at just the right spice level after I asked for it "da la" 大麻) with four ingredients. 

I chose vermicelli, broccoli, Chinese cabbage, and tofu rolls. Each was super tasty! I was a little disappointed as I had asked the friendly cashier/server if the tofu roll was the crispy beancurd roll from hotpot and he said yes, but it wasn't. It was more like rolled tofu skin--still tasty though. The broccoli was fresh and perfectly cooked, and the cabbage soaked up the delicious mala oils while the vermicelli provided the "umph" to the dish. The soup came topped with sliced spring onions and a generous dollop of chili sauce with sesame seeds. 

A little while into our meal, the server came over with two cups of mung bean soup for my friend and I. He said the chef had decided to make up a batch and gave it to us on the house. It was a nice balance of sweet to match the spicy soup, and had a liberal serving of perfectly cooked mung beans at the bottom. 

The only downside was the place's lack of nice utensils. Though I ordered my meal "for here," it came with a small plastic spoon and disposable wooden chopsticks. I carry my own chopsticks, so no worries there (BYO people, be green!), but the spoon was sad. I want to eat a lovely soup like that with a proper Chinese soup spoon that has room for me to arrange the perfect bite with my chopsticks. The silly little plastic one just didn't cut it. Despite that, I thoroughly enjoyed my lunch, and ate every bite. I think I'll be back; maybe I can try the jian bing (Chinese crepe) next time.'}, {'review_stars': 3.0, 'review_date': '2019-11-13 02:26:06', 'review_text': "The food here is ok but not great, and kind of expensive for what it is. We tried both the mala tang and the dry pot (peanut-based sauce). The base is free, and you pay for the items you chose to add in (minimum of 4). They assemble your hot pot or dry hot pot in the kitchen before bringing it out in a regular bowl. The mala soup base was not very numbing, and the flavors of the base didn't seem very incorporated into the mix-ins.

They also have jian bing and some appetizers, which we didn't try--some of their other items may be better."}, {'review_stars': 3.0, 'review_date': '2019-05-28 02:06:56', 'review_text': 'I believe the restaurant will not last long, even their food is not bad, because the price is much higher than expected.'}]}

{'name': 'The Ritz-Carlton Bacara, Santa Barbara', 'address': '8301 Hollister Ave', 'city': 'Santa Barbara', 'state': 'CA', 'categories': 'Restaurants, Beauty & Spas, American (New), Event Planning & Services, Hotels, Massage, Resorts, Day Spas, Hotels & Travel, Venues & Event Spaces', 'hours': {'Monday': '0:0-0:0', 'Tuesday': '0:0-0:0', 'Wednesday': '0:0-0:0', 'Thursday': '0:0-0:0', 'Friday': '0:0-0:0', 'Saturday': '0:0-0:0', 'Sunday': '0:0-0:0'}, 'attributes': {'BusinessParking': {'garage': False, 'street': False, 'validated': False, 'lot': False, 'valet': True}, 'RestaurantsReservations': None, 'OutdoorSeating': None, 'WiFi': 'free', 'RestaurantsTakeOut': None, 'RestaurantsGoodForGroups': None, 'Music': None, 'Ambience': None}, 'business_stars': 3.5, 'review_info': [{'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2022-01-18 10:48:02', 'review_text': 'Beautiful setting. Nice addition to the chain. Great service by Jon! Food was good. And the ambiance was nice too.'}, {'review_stars': 3.0, 'review_date': '2022-01-04 18:37:34', 'review_text': "Only a solid THREE STARS. 

I will put in details for my review here. This review is not biased, pure 100%  our experience staying at Ritz Carlton Bacara Santa Barbara. 

Ritz Carlton SB is not located in a prime area. This is a resort,  their property is isolated away from downtown so that customers can enjoy the activities that the resort provides, such as hiking by the beach, biking around the hotel area. Mind you, this hotel is not walkable to restaurants in SB, they provided their own chain restaurants to make guests enjoy and relax at the property.

Here is our experience:
Like any other experienced travellers, we booked our hotels through credit card partners using our mileage. Who pay their hotel rooms in full nowadays anyways? Most smart people pay their hotel rooms with points or mileage affiliated with credit cards company. Originally we booked for double beds. However a day before, I received an email from them that my room was changed to single bed. This is quite impossible to our situation since we have 2 adults and 2 children. So I called (many times) to our credit card company and to Ritz to make sure we would get 2 double beds instead of 1 single king bed. The number of phone calls to change the number of beds did not happen only in one call, unfortunately. I had to call them several times before our arrival. 

So, arrival day, we got our 2 double beds. Perfect. Checking in was a smooth process. The receptionist was polite, explaining us everything about the resort. By the way, the resort has so many activities that you could book first through their website. And I also appreciate the concierge emailed us days before reminding us about the activities on the days we stay. (Tips: the receptionist offered us a transportation like a luggage cart to transport the guests to your villas, ACCEPT IT! Ritz carlton property is so huge that you can even get lost in the area. You can walk if you want to reach your 10k steps, though). 

When we got to our hotel, problem arise. Tv did not work. I called the receptionist, and the technician came after an hour later. They tried their best to fix tv, unfortunately the problem was much bigger than they expected. The sad part was they KNEW the tv was broken already and Ritz didn't even solve the problem. (Technician told me our previous guest also had the same problem - broken tv). So, me just being me, asking for a compensation for the broken tv. The technician was very generous giving us $100 restaurant fee.  And I took it with my grateful heart. 

Resort wise; this property again has a lot activities that you can do with your kids, partners, etc. Hiking by the beach is the most popular activities done by my people. Biking around the area also a great thing to do. 

During check out time, there was a bit problem since I wanted to redeem the $100 certificate that the technician gave me. A bit miscommunication happened at that time, but they resolved it quickly.

Would I recommend you guys to stay here? if you have $1000 extra to spare for staying a night here, sure you should. 
Don't forget to add
Parking vallet is $53 a day
Resort fee is $50 a day. (that's why maximise the property since you're charged hefty for resort fee). 

Other than that, yes I would stay again here. Hopefully I can bump this review to 4 star next time."}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2021-12-28 21:00:04', 'review_text': 'Amazing hotel with such such comfy rooms. We had a iv and there was an area right outside the room where he could go in the morning..so so convenient..there was a small lawn with a gorgeous fountain right outside the room which was awesome. The GYM WAS BEYOND FANTASTIC!!! the views were great. Service was great at the coffee shop and the bar. Room service we got it in the lobby was great. THE YUCCA FRIES ARE A MUST Have!!! The chips and guacamole were totally yum! We got takeout in the lobby and hung out which was so fun! Having a fridge and microwave in the room was so helpful. We will definitely come back. Highly recommend!'}]}

context:
{'name': "Neighbor Tim's BBQ", 'address': '152 -E Aero Camino', 'city': 'Goleta', 'state': 'CA', 'categories': 'Food Trucks, Barbeque, Restaurants, Food Delivery Services, Food, American (New)', 'hours': {'Thursday': '16:45-20:0', 'Friday': '16:45-20:0', 'Saturday': '16:45-20:0', 'Sunday': '16:45-20:0'}, 'attributes': {'BusinessParking': {'garage': False, 'street': True, 'validated': False, 'lot': False, 'valet': False}, 'RestaurantsReservations': False, 'OutdoorSeating': False, 'WiFi': 'no', 'RestaurantsTakeOut': True, 'RestaurantsGoodForGroups': None, 'Music': None, 'Ambience': None}, 'business_stars': 4.5, 'review_info': [{'review_stars': 3.0, 'review_date': '2020-10-11 05:12:36', 'review_text': 'I had been wanting to try Neighbor Tim's BBQ since I heard about it several months ago. Tonight was finally the night we were going to order it! I ordered through GrubHub - I got the 2 meats/1 side deal with 2 orders of brisket, Mac and cheese, and added a side of the potato salad with bacon, and the beans with bacon. That also comes with white bread, pickles, and their "famous BBQ sauce". Overall, it was a little pricey but IF it's good I am willing to pay it. 

So, I am really bummed out to report that I was really disappointed by the food. I wanted to like it so much I thought for sure this would be my new go-to BBQ place! But, one of the biggest things was they didn't give me any of their "famous BBQ sauce" and that's really what felt like was missing from the brisket. 

The sides were ok. I thought the Mac and cheese was a little dry/bland but with a little salt and pepper on it, it helped. I thought the potato salad with bacon was pretty good but needed something acidic to break down all the starchiness. The beans with bacon was good - probably my favorite side of the 3.
I'd be willing to give it another try in the future WITH their BBQ sauce though, so don't totally count me out!'}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2020-09-27 04:52:17', 'review_text': "Loved our family feast! Thank you for bringing the joy of smoked meats and carefully crafted sides to our Saturday night. Awesome brisket and mac and cheese. Glad we decided to make the last minute addition of corn and black bean salad. Amazing customer service.  Thank you Tim's crew! We'll be ordering again."}, {'review_stars': 4.0, 'review_date': '2020-09-25 02:53:03', 'review_text': "One of the good things about having grown up nephews and nieces: people of whom i have had a good amount of contact and influence on when they were growing up: adults now who drive, think, analyze, care about you (as in me) is that i can ask them to drive me somewhere, get me something at various and sundry places and as in last week: order, pay for and pick up a meal. To be fair i warned them when they were kids: your uncle is going to expect to be paid back for all the driving, picking up, leaving off, etc., etc., i did for you. So NOW is that time.
But for now i am relishing the well earned attention. And i'm only 1/2 kidding here in any event.
Several weeks ago my niece told me about Neighbor Tim's both as a food truck and a stand alone BBQ joint.
I zipped into SB and asked her to bring some BBQ for me, her kids and hubby as well as for my sisters. She raved about this place. So i thought: ok, sounds good: so bring me some, please.
Well bring me some she did:
Pork ribs
Brisket
Potato Salad
Mac and cheese
White Bread thick sliced
Sauce: Amazing Sauce it is called
Burnt Ends (from another time but reviewed here)
The ribs were perfectly cooked and meaty. I am a purist when it comes to ribs. they must be tender and juicy but not falling off the bone meaning i pick one up and the meat falls off. That is too well cooked, usually cooked with too much moisture and not enough heat or smoke.
These ribs though were well smoked, well seasoned (not too salty) and were delivered w/o sauce which is the way i prefer them so that you can taste the meat and not just the sauce. The sauce btw is super also: vinegary, light on the sugars with a snappy chili finish: north carolina style which surprised me only because i thought this was a Texas style BBQ joint. But it works so why fight it. 
The Brisket was also well seasoned and smoked as well as very moist and tender. No knife needed to cut and we made sammys using the white bread and dipping in the sauce to finish.
The Mac and cheese is nothing special: just cheese sauce and noodles. This needed salt and pepper for starters. 
The potato salad was a big letdown also: the dressing was good and in the right proportion: neither too much or not enough but again salt and pepper would have helped and acid is needed desperately. Pickles, mustard, maybe or both?  Also most damning is that the potatoes were slightly undercooked. in other words too al dente for my taste. 
Thick white bread was in the Texas toast style and it was real tasty: reminded me of a traditional pullman loaf. Yeasty, high quality bread not Wonder Bread, remember?
The burnt ends were not part of this order but we had these about 4 weeks ago and they were absolutely first rate: charred, and well seasoned to within an inch of their lives. Tim's are made from the deckle of the brisket i am going to assume and are super tasty chunks of beefy goodness. Next day you can thinly slice and use in a taco or sammy or just to pop in your mouth while pretending to grab a beer from the refer.
All in all a very good sampling today of Neighbor Tim's wares:  what they do well they do very, very well. I will be back!"}]}

It was a touch more watery than I would have liked.  I also did not come away with a nice rich, buttery flavor; this offering was much less nuanced, pedestrian in nature.  Clam was seldom seen throughout.  The oysters were definitely a miss.  These were not the famous ones from Antoine's.  The variation here was a very thick layer of melted cheese spiked with spinach or was that chives?  I couldn't tell, because the only thing I could taste was cheese.  It even dominated the natural taste of the ocean in the oysters entirely which really misses the point of the dish.  It is called Rockefeller because it is a very rich dish.  This was clunky.  Worse, one could simply scrape off the layer of coagulated cheese and just eat the oyster.  There was no melding of flavors, no subtleness to it at all. {'name': 'Soho Restaurant & Music Club', 'address': '1221 State St, Ste 205', 'city': 'Santa Barbara', 'state': 'CA', 'categories': 'Music Venues, Bars, Nightlife, Arts & Entertainment, Restaurants, American (Traditional)', 'hours': {'Monday': '0:0-0:0', 'Tuesday': '18:30-23:0', 'Wednesday': '18:30-23:0', 'Thursday': '18:30-23:0', 'Friday': '18:30-2:0', 'Saturday': '18:30-2:0', 'Sunday': '18:30-23:0'}, 'attributes': {'BusinessParking': {'garage': True, 'street': True, 'validated': False, 'lot': False, 'valet': False}, 'RestaurantsReservations': True, 'OutdoorSeating': True, 'WiFi': 'no', 'RestaurantsTakeOut': True, 'RestaurantsGoodForGroups': True, 'Music': True, 'Ambience': {'touristy': False, 'hipster': None, 'romantic': False, 'divey': False, 'intimate': None, 'trendy': None, 'upscale': False, 'classy': None, 'casual': True}}, 'business_stars': 3.5, 'review_info': [{'review_stars': 1.0, 'review_date': '2022-01-12 17:40:26', 'review_text': "called us idiots. we're extremely rude and racist. went to have a good time but clearly i didn't."}, {'review_stars': 1.0, 'review_date': '2022-01-09 01:50:53', 'review_text': 'I tested positive for COVID (despite being vaccinated and boosted) and even though I am feeling fine, I made the responsible decision to not attend a show at Soho I had purchased tickets to in advance.  

Unfortunately, their no refund policy encourages people who have tested positive to attend shows anyway, increasing the chance of spreading COVID, when cases are currently surging in Santa Barbara County with the highly contagious omicron variant.

I understand that a no refund policy for event tickets may be standard in normal circumstances, but many, if not all, concert venues in town have adapted flexible and generous refund/exchange policies for obvious reasons in the current climate.'}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2021-12-31 15:51:19', 'review_text': "Bottom Line:  The best live music venue in Santa Barbara, without question.  I think we are in House of Blues territory now!

Toe's Tavern, The Hibar, Yukitan, The Coach House, The history of Santa Barbara music venues is nearly as colorful as the history of the bands that came out of this town; Toad the Wet Sproket, Dishwalla, Big Bad Voodoo Daddy, The Ataris, Lagwagon, No Use for a Name, Snot, Nerf Herder......the SBCC Lunch Break band.

Nearly every single live music venue is gone from the golden era of music in this town.  Back in the 1990's I used to be on the local 'sound guys' that would turn up and try and make sure people could hear the bands.

Soho was absolutely one of the places where I used to do sound and sit against the wall behind what was basically a TV tray with a Mackie 1604 on it for $40 a night plus free drinks.  It was dark and kind of dingy but thats what they were all like back in the world of analog.

How times have changed in 20+ years (which is essentially the amount of time its been since i have been to Soho).

Ive heard people talk about it recently but never put much thought into it as I used to work there - still had the idea of a dark, hole in the wall type place in my brain.

Well, last night the wife tells me that we have a date night.  Sweet!   It had been some time since we had been able to get out together.  I didnt know that local hero Chris Schiflett was going to be playing so having dinner with the wife was going to be icing on the cake for sure.

We had the hamburger and the raviolis both of which were great.  The burger was excellent which frankly is really saying something with the burger game being what it is these days.  Tender, moist, lots of flavor - steak fries done properly which is a little crispy and not doughy and hot enough to burn my mouth!   Yes!!
The raviolis are very similar to the ones I have had at Ca'Dario and were excellent.

Over drinks we had a Creme Brule as well and that was also delicious.

Into the room for the show and do you know how you can tell how the sound is pretty good?  When you dont notice it.

While getting my audio engineering degree years ago, I realized that the best sound people are ones that you never notice.  Which is kind of sad but it absolutely true - Ive always said that a good sound are like good windshield wipers: if they are doing their job properly, you'll never know they are there.

Half way through the show, I realized that I had just been enjoying the show and not critiquing how it sounded and it hit me - thats because the sound was good!  How refreshing and thank you kind engineer, whomever you are.


I was properly blown away last night at our visit to Soho.  Food was great, the venue is great, the staff are helpful, the bathrooms are modern and functional, the sound was great....  I may not have even recognized it had I been teleported - they have done an incredible job right down to the super modern and very fancy bathrooms.

Ill be back here just for dinner - not even for a show but I will absolutely be back for both. 

Well done, Soho!  You're keeping the local music scene alive"}]}

The food came pretty quickly but unfortunately for me it wreaked havoc on my stomach. Within one hour of finishing my food I found myself sitting on a public toilet in downtown Santa Blahblah between a movie theater and a Marshells. The porcelain toilet shone in the way that only a toilet used by countless homeless men, as not only a receptacle for their excrement but also a handy place to nap, could shine. That being said when I left that not so holy of throne rooms it was stained with with the dark blood of man who hasn't been comfortable on a toilet for far too long."}, {'review_stars': 1.0, 'review_date': '2015-05-18 17:26:02', 'review_text': "My fiancee and I frequented Killer B's at their State street location, and I would have given it a 3.5 star at that time.  It was always so-so. A good place to grab a quick drink, or a quick bite when you want something quick and easy, nothing fancy.  The servers were always rather annoying and not very friendly or quick, and they always spent more time talking to each other than to their tables. 

We finally decided to try out the new Killer B location with the intent of using our Axxess card there, and we left without even ordering food.  

We sat at a table inside, and was greeted by the bartender who was EXTREMELY rude and unfriendly.  He literally walked away from me mid sentence. He tried everything possible to avoid eye contact, and he had absolutely not hustle in his step. That however wasn't even the last straw.  We went ahead and ordered two drinks, a beer for me and a cosmopolitan for the lady. the cosmo was GROSS! I honestly think that he forgot to put he alcohol in the drink.  It was watered down and tasteless.  

Bad service, bad drinks... we decided not to continue and order what we can only assume would be bad food. 

Will never be returning here by my own decision.  Too many other good restaurants to waste time on this place.  We walked over the Santa Barbara Brew and enjoyed a delicious dinner and drinks that actually had alcohol in them."}, {'review_stars': 1.0, 'review_date': '2015-05-10 21:37:39', 'review_text': "Such a shame!

This review is not based on the food at this establishment, rather on the lack of service and attention.

I was in Santa Barbara for the day on the weekend of Cinco de Mayo. It was a Sunday around 2pm and I had been looking for a place to eat when I searched for a BBQ joint nearby.

When I got to the place I walked into the courtyard where a band was setting up and a few people were gathered.

As I walked into the place there was a sign that read something like please wait to be seated.

The girl behind the bar was talking with some other girl and when I asked whether I could sit anywhere there was no response, not even a glance.

The possible owner of the place (An older leathery looking fellow) walked in and ushered a server outside to do something and again, no attention to the new walk in what so ever.

Finally I asked the guy behind the line what's going on and he finally told me that they are offering a Cinco de Mayo menu and no BBQ in sight.

So... this place get a 1 star from me and a promise that I will NEVER come back again!

When I worked in the restaurant business back in NYC there was NO SUCH THING as letting a walk in just stand there unattended within 30 seconds or less. At least acknowledge the person and let them feel like you gave a S!

I had high hopes for this place... alas!"}]}

I asked for their names and they responded "I don't have to tell you shit". Worst night of my life. I went home crying after the horrible stuff that was said to me. Sharkeez right next door was way more accommodating, if your looking for a safe bar with friendly people, I would recommended Sharkeez.'}, {'review_stars': 1.0, 'review_date': '2021-11-15 17:44:26', 'review_text': "My son was a groomsman at a wedding in Santa Barbara on October 10, 2021, a Sunday night.  After the reception, around 11:30 pm, a couple of the guests went to O'Malleys for a drink.  My son's bar bill was $40 and he added a $15 tip.  O'Malleys' added $400 to his credit card for a total of a $455 charge.  This is FRAUD and THEFT.  I called OMalleys' numerous times, leaving a name and phone number since they don't answer their telephone - with no reply.  I am shocked and dismayed by this outright theft.  DO NOT PATRONIZE THIS BAR, their employees are dishonest and thieves."}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2021-08-11 23:42:10', 'review_text': 'I know, I know.... People might be shocked to see that O'Malley's gets a five star rating from me.   More on that later.  

O'Malley's is hard to categorize which I believe is the reason it is not carrying a higher Yelp rating.   I would describe it as a "neighborhood bar located at ground zero of tourist central".  Some would call it a dive bar, it isn't.   I am a bit of a dive bar connoisseur and O'Malley's falls short or more accurately elevates above the standard of dive bar.   Mostly for the lack of bar flys sitting at the counter at 11:00am.  

They have an amazing team, many who have worked here for years and are loved by SB locals.   Duane and Katie are fabulous and today as I write this Haley is working!   She just made me an amazing margarita.  

Now, why five stars? Because I come here so freakin' much.   How could I ever knock off a star for a place I frequent many times in a month.  SB falls short in the sports bar department and O'Malleys has TV's in abundance!  Inside, on the patio and now out front on State Street.   Whether you love football, Dodger games or European Soccer they have it on.   If you are visiting SB you might have noticed it is not uncommon to pay $16 for a drink, not O'Malley's, I don't know how many times I got my bill and said "we ordered five drinks" and heard them respond "yes, they are all on there".  As a tourist you might find O'Malley's to be a little unkept for the fancy SB vibe you are used to, but come back on your next visit, and then your next.   I'm sure you will embrace it as I do.'}]}

context:
{'name': 'La Casa De Maria', 'address': '800 El Bosque Rd', 'city': 'Santa Barbara', 'state': 'CA', 'categories': 'Health Retreats, Venues & Event Spaces, Hotels & Travel, Restaurants, Event Planning & Services', 'hours': {'Monday': '8:0-23:0', 'Tuesday': '8:0-23:0', 'Wednesday': '8:0-23:0', 'Thursday': '8:0-23:0', 'Friday': '8:0-23:0', 'Saturday': '8:0-23:0', 'Sunday': '8:0-23:0'}, 'attributes': {'BusinessParking': None, 'RestaurantsReservations': None, 'OutdoorSeating': None, 'WiFi': None, 'RestaurantsTakeOut': None, 'RestaurantsGoodForGroups': None, 'Music': None, 'Ambience': None}, 'business_stars': 4.5, 'review_info': [{'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2018-01-10 17:43:18', 'review_text': "I love this place. Would stay here forever, if I could. Accommodations are a little rustic, but that's part of the charm. Lovely place for self-discovery and contemplation."}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2017-08-05 01:28:13', 'review_text': "What a beautiful place and relaxing atmosphere. The accommodations are fairly comfortable, but it's the grounds that make this gem of a place special. (The food doesn't hurt either.) Get a massage from Vesalina if at all possible; it was the best I've ever had. Also, be sure to check out the gardens and chapels."}, {'review_stars': 1.0, 'review_date': '2017-04-15 17:16:29', 'review_text': 'i write this review for two reasons. 1) The welfare of future guests; 2) and because the issue has never been addressed by anyone at the facility since I packed my things, left a day before the end of my $piritual retreat, and proceeded to spend the very expensive day delousing everything I owned -- including detailing my car, fumigating/washing/drycleaning all my clothes that I didn't want to throw away -- before returning home to my girlfriend and the beautiful bed she bought a few months before..

Bedbugs. Repeat. Bedbugs, In my case, Room 12.  Infested. I slept in the shower stall the last night I was there because sadly this was not my first rodeo with bedbugs. Note: I am not thin-skinned when it comes to the outdoors. I have lived out of a tent for several summers in Alaska. I love hiking, camping, and grew up in a rural area in the midwest. In short, I am not spooked by a raccoon (or a bear for that matter) or run for cover if buzzed by a hornet. 

But bedbugs?   (yeeeych) 

Yes, those wonderful little traveling insects -- much like fleas -- that want to come home with you. The kind of bugs that bite and bite -- in my case, my neck and legs -- and seemingly can live beyond any pesticide or laundering. 

I would rather have mice running across my face than knowingly sleep in a bed(room) infested with bedbugs. 

To be fair, the young man working the desk the night I "captured" one of the well-sized creatures in a plastic cup was sympathetic and saw about getting me another room the next morning.  I was so itchy and disappointed that I had to deal with the after effects of being bedbugged, I supplied all my contact info and left immediately. That is the last I have heard from Casa de la Maria despite 3 emails and 2 phone calls. (I was asking for cleaning restitution and the two nights I was in Room 12 comped to me and that was all.)

The grounds? Hey, it's Montecito/San Ysidro Ranch country. It's gorgeou$!  And makes Santa Barbara look like a suburb of Bakersfield...And the staff was friendly and mostly helpful. The fact that they booked a rather loud and demanding group of guests midway through a silent Buddhist retreat? Eh, I get it.  Co-exist, people.  Just not w/ bedbugs.'}]}

As of today, I have not had a response via email or phone. 

That certainly sums it all up, unfortunately.'}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2021-12-31 02:04:39', 'review_text': 'Rustys is great for family and quick family dinners. A little salty so you will be up all night but the pizza is definitely worth it!'}, {'review_stars': 1.0, 'review_date': '2021-12-27 23:44:13', 'review_text': "So, I placed an order last night with Rusty's off of Carrillo for delivery, as I had many times over the last decades (SB local). I was VERY specific in my delivery details, as my house is behind the main house on the property. I am unit A. About an hour later, my neighbor knocked on my door and was holding my pizza. He told me that he found it on his back porch. I was stunned, but, thankful that he found it. 

I took the pizza inside and when I opened the box, the pizza was cold (no surprise) and there was a piece missing. Yep, somebody had eaten a piece of it. I know it wasn't my neighbor, which means it was likely the driver (?), or maybe it was done at the pizza joint and he didn't check the box?? Either way, I was completely disgusted. We are in the middle of a pandemic and somebody touched/ate my food.  UGH. 

I called and spoke with a supervisor (Jonathan) and told him the story and told him that the pizza was on my front porch, as I refused to eat it and refused to order a replacement (I just didn't trust them not to mess with my food.) The driver came back and grabbed the pizza and I am supposed to be getting a refund. We'll see if that really happens. 

I will not order food from them again, which makes me sad. I have a 40+ year history of patronage at Rustys, but, this is not okay. Too bad."}]}

We had an amazing time, the food was delicious!!! We had appetizer soup /salad main chorus and dessert. our waitress was amazing (sorry I forgot her name)!!, the band was great. They let me bring a cake and we celebrate my daughter birthday right after midnight. Highly Highly recommend , thank you again for an amazing night , what a great way to start the year'}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2021-12-21 18:08:39', 'review_text': "My daughter wanted a large wedding filled with family and friends but, COVID made that impossible right now. She and her fiance really wanted to be married and so, they decided to have a simple private wedding on the lawn of the Santa Barbara Courthouse with dinner afterwards at Opal restaurant. Only the my daughter, her fiance, my husband, our other daughter and our grandchildren were there. I wanted to do something to help make this day memorable and special for them. I reached out to Richard, owner and event coordinator of Opal. Over a few weeks and with many emails between us, Richard and I put together what was simply the best mini reception the newlyweds could have had and, somehow we managed to keep it a secret.\nFrom suggestions for appetizers, to working out details of timing, to having his staff find a way to separate us from the other diners in their cozy and comfortable parklet (the restaurant was packed) and set a beautiful table with a floral centerpiece and rose petals I provided, Richard showed great understanding and appreciation of what I was trying to accomplish. He's a truly kind and caring man. To top it all off, the food was spectacular! The service was outstanding! I would recommend Opal Restaurant highly for any special event and absolutely for a lovely and delicious dinner."}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2021-12-16 16:21:24', 'review_text': 'Went here before a show at the theater next door. We said we were seeing a show in an hour. Ordered food and it was coming out a few minutes later. Great food, great service, great outdoor seating!!'}]}

Hot, fluffy, and crispy. Oh my sugary goodness, I'm salivating as I am writing this review. LOL. Portion size was quite generous for the price. I recommend eating them fresh! 10/10 recommend!"}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2021-09-24 17:28:27', 'review_text': 'Best churros in town! perfect for events or even as a grab n go if you stop by at their location. A super friendly and family owned business. these churros never fail'}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2021-09-15 17:44:42', 'review_text': 'In celebration of Latinx Heritage Month, my sweet tooth couldn't help itself but to share quite the hidden treasure in Santa Barbara:
Fernando's Churros.
Off a Westside side street...
behind Rosale's Mexican Restaurant and Auto Zone...
in a vacant parking lot...
sits the bright orange Fernando's Churros truck. 

Bring those bills, henny!! 
Once you get a whiff of the fresh churro dough blended with cinnamon and sugar, you'll be crunching it like it's hot!

Fernando is super sweet and easy going. Once I didn't have cash and his card reader wasn't working--he said to just come back another day to pay him. Doesn't get more trustworthy and local than that! 

Their truck slogan reads: "PRUEBA--DISFRUTA--REPITE! (Try...Enjoy...Repeat!)". Just like that, you'll be an addict and regular like myself! 
Oh, heck, he knew I'd be back for my fix. 

Fernando also does catering for large events (quinceañeras, weddings, etc) with the toppings (chocolate and caramel sauce), plus offers local delivery!
Note: Keep in mind that they're there only in Santa Barbara Mondays and Thursdays but travel to Lompoc, Vandenberg and Lompoc the remaining days of the week.

Since I'm such a Churro fan, check out where else in Santa Barbara County to get your churro on--though none can triumph Fernando's!
https://www.yelp.com/collection/Y3ZRTDE_Iu9CUfDN3Dq4FA?utm_content=Collections&utm_source=ishare'}]}

Right way to end a holiday weekend."}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2022-01-12 23:49:00', 'review_text': "A Santa Barbara staple! I used to go to the downtown location, but after that one closed, I haven't been to Jeannine's since. Man, have I missed it!

You order at the counter and then find a table either indoors or outdoors. The space isn't too big, so if you're with a large group, it may be difficult to find seating. Staff was super friendly.

If you're a sweet brunch person, the banana Kahlua French toast is a MUST! The challah bread is fluffy and not too eggy as some french toasts can be, and the caramelized, Kahlua glazed bananas on top are sweet goodness. The Norwegian Benedict was nice and light with the smoked salmon, tomatoes, and cucumbers. The breakfast burrito was too heavy for me though. I loved the grilled tortilla, but the fillings alone were quite mushy. The crispy home fries on the side and salsa were much needed to add a bit of crunch and to cut the richness of the cheesy eggs and bacon. Nonetheless, Jeannine's honestly has a special place in my heart, and it's because of that banana Kahlua French toast!"}, {'review_stars': 4.0, 'review_date': '2022-01-12 05:15:32', 'review_text': "Jeannine's is a nice breakfast and brunch spot across from the wharf with ocean views. I ordered a simple two egg breakfast with toast and fresh squeezed orange juice.  I placed a to go order and enjoyed my breakfast in the sand! 

The staff were very welcoming and quickly took my order. While I waited, I noticed how cute and quaint this place is. I was quickly served and left to the beach to enjoy. I would say my breakfast was about 3.5 and the juice and service was a 5! My eggs and potatoes were meh and sourdough was good. I think next time I will try a pastry (that seems to be the thing to get at Jeannine's)."}]}

Super fresh, delicious and inventive. The squash blossom quesadilla was delicious (the blue corn tortilla was out of this world). The staff was also super friendly."}, {'review_stars': 2.0, 'review_date': '2022-01-15 20:41:34', 'review_text': 'Pretty disappointing. I\'ve been wanting to eat here for several years based on what I thought was a great reputation in town.  I don\'t get over to the Mesa much but when I saw a coupon in a mailed flyer a couple months ago, I clipped it.  It offered a free entree with purchase of one entree and two drinks. Even with a discount of $12, the bill was $53 for lunch (before tip) -- I can eat a great dinner out for less than this -- so I was expecting a very delicious meal.  Both my companion and I felt the food was vastly overpriced for the quality, flavor and even presentation. The quantity was generous enough but the items we ordered (empanadas and the mushroom burger) were both rather bland.  The "burger" was served on a doughy, barely cooked pita-like bread rather than on an appropriate item to hold a messy burger in place like a brioche bun.  It was completely falling apart after a few bites... which would have been okay to eat with a fork except it was just not flavorful or complex given the list of ingredients. The most interesting item was the ketchup made from beets.  Also the side fries weren\'t thoroughly cooked except for the smallest ones.  Definitely not a $24 item! The empanadas were somewhat better but again, not a \'wow\' and not even a \'hey, really tasty.\' Just bland and again, not worth $24.  The half-off discount didn\'t even cover the $13 cost of a chai tea and lemonade (that was mostly ice of course).  All in all, very disappointing food and therefore completely overpriced.  We took the food to go so I cannot really comment on the service. When I read another lengthy review (12/5/21), I now see that the prices have been jacked and the quality has been wavering. I totally understand the precarious state of the restaurant business right now during covid but I cannot recommend this restaurant at this time.'}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2022-01-10 16:46:12', 'review_text': 'Flavors are always spot on! Service is also good. Best fusion snd vegan food in Santa Barbara.'}]}

{'name': 'Petit Valentien', 'address': '1114 State St, Ste 14', 'city': 'Santa Barbara', 'state': 'CA', 'categories': 'Restaurants, Ethiopian, French', 'hours': {'Monday': '0:0-0:0', 'Tuesday': '17:0-20:30', 'Wednesday': '16:30-19:30', 'Thursday': '17:30-20:0', 'Friday': '17:30-20:0', 'Saturday': '17:30-21:0', 'Sunday': '17:0-20:30'}, 'attributes': {'BusinessParking': {'garage': True, 'street': True, 'validated': False, 'lot': False, 'valet': False}, 'RestaurantsReservations': True, 'OutdoorSeating': True, 'WiFi': 'no', 'RestaurantsTakeOut': True, 'RestaurantsGoodForGroups': True, 'Music': None, 'Ambience': {'touristy': False, 'hipster': False, 'romantic': True, 'divey': False, 'intimate': True, 'trendy': False, 'upscale': False, 'classy': True, 'casual': False}}, 'business_stars': 4.0, 'review_info': [{'review_stars': 1.0, 'review_date': '2021-12-12 04:20:43', 'review_text': 'Husband and I walked by Petit Valentien this afternoon and the Ethiopian lunch dishes we saw on the patio looked great, so we stopped in for dinner about 30 minutes ago. We weren't in a huge rush, but we didn't have hours to dine, because I had dough proofing at home and needed to attend to it. Hubby walked in and checked in with waitress and came out saying we should take any seat we wanted and she'd be right out with menus. So, we sat next to the Xmas tree on the patio and waited... and waited... and waited.

We figured she couldn't see us so we moved right next to the front door, where she couldn't miss us. After waiting several minutes, I decided we would wait 5 more minutes and then find somewhere else for dinner. After 3 minutes, a 30-something brunette waitress came and dropped off menus. I let her know the table was wobbly and she said she'd bring a block for it (which she never did). Oddly, I detected a twinge of attitude from the waitress with that exchange... not sure why, but there was an element of nastiness in her tone. She also turned on the table-side heater, which was very effective, if somewhat atmosphere-killing, because of its very bright coils.

I pulled up Yelp to look at the pics of the entrees and we settled on what we wanted to order. After about 5 more minutes, the waitress returned and informed us that dinner would take longer here than other restaurants. I let her know I was proofing dough at home and if we ordered immediately, how long would it take. She replied that if we ordered faster, the food would come faster. (Duh! Crazy how that works!) Again, a twinge of nastiness.

Hubby and I heard the specials and I asked her to repeat the sides on one of the specials. Attitude was ramping up from her. I asked her what her favorite item on the menu was and she replied, "I can't say. You're the one who's going to eat it, not me! The chef wouldn't even tell you." 

I didn't dare ask another question, because she was in full beeeyatch mode. We both ordered off the regular menu. Then, she said it would be 20 minutes and we ordered wine. Before she left, I asked the waitress if she could please ask the chef not to hold the entree until we finished the appetizer... just to put it all in at once. Her response? "The chef doesn't need to be micromanaged like that." And that, my Yelp friends, was my limit to the abuse I was going to take from this witch of a waitress.

I smiled and said thank you, but we aren't going to stay. She said okay, opened the door, walked into the restaurant, and I could hear her behind me exclaiming with a groan as she entered, "Oh my Gaaaaawwwdd."

Good thing they're in a tourist zone, because Petit Valentien can have a fresh supply of customers the waitress can abuse. As for this local, I'd recommend one of our other many fine dining establishments and give this one a wide berth, if service is important to you.

Two last comments: First, we noticed the restaurant had a Help Wanted sign posted, looking for servers, which could hint that maybe the attitude starts at the top and they have a hard time keeping servers. Second, I noticed another one of the local Yelp Elite crew (and other reviewers) also commented on the rude and inattentive service here. Seems to be a trend and unfortunately, it was our unlucky turn to experience it tonight.'}, {'review_stars': 1.0, 'review_date': '2021-10-08 16:53:58', 'review_text': "Just don't go. They are severely understaffed and incredibly rude. We had to walk out after the waitress ignored us for about 15-20 minutes. We had to ask her three times to let us know what the wait time would be. Very unprofessional."}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2021-10-02 22:38:26', 'review_text': 'Amazing experience with a variety of Ethiopian flavors giving it the right touch. A story in flavors you need to experience. Lovely location feels like your in another world.  
Great service.'}]}

I often confuse this spot with Mizza and the adjoining 1114 restaurant. To my knowledge, they are under the same ownership. I order through Grubhub as all three spots offer crazy coupon discounts. Delivery, tax, and the driver fee will rack the price way back up so I opted to pick up from the location today. 

The restaurant is named after their buy one, get one free pizzas. I've tried and they are amazing! Great value. Tonight, I ordered a burger which is the same one cooked at 1114. It's large, full of flavor, and comes with a hearty serving of seasoned fries. 

BOGO is located within 1114. As soon as you enter the doors, take an immediate left where you will be greeted by a white counter with their logo. They appear to operate separately from the main restaurant. I was immediately helped by 3 different employees and appreciate how efficient they all were. My food was warm and packaged well!

I added lettuce and raw onions to my house burger and it was delicious. The chipotle sauce and angus burger patty combination went straight to my tummy and their beer batters fries adds a good texture. The flourless chocolate cake had a thick fudge consistency- which I like but flavor wise was ok. When I placed the order they suggested a light lemon cream filling which I went with. It was beyond perfect. Light fluffy buttercream, moist cake, and the filling was a perfect match up. Yep, I will be back!'}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2014-08-07 19:34:34', 'review_text': 'Katie baked the wedding cake of my dreams. It came out exactly as I wanted and was delicious. We had three tiers: two were red velvet and one was vanilla custard cake with fresh raspberries. I am very happy with our decision to choose her.'}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2014-05-09 15:00:38', 'review_text': 'Katie Teall is an ANGEL!!!  I made a wedding cake for the daughter of a very close High School friend and the Fondant I bought (FondArt) was horrible.  I spent about 7 hours kneading it and rolling it, and no matter what I did when I covered a tier of the cake it cracked.  I was so stressed that decided to go to Montecito Confections to see if I could buy Fondant there.  I got there and asked Katie Teall the sweet adorable, British owner if she could help me.  she explained that she sold the store and the rest of her Fondant was for the last 15 wedding cakes that she had to deliver during the summer.  She must have seen my face of stress, exhaustion, just lost, and asked:  How big is your cake?  I told her and she said:  Do you want me to cover it in Fondant for you?  I jumped to hug her and kiss her and hug her and kissed her some more.

I came home and the mother of the bride carried the bottom tier and we went back to Montecito Confections to be saved by Katie.  We arrived and she said where is the rest of the cake?  I did not want to take advantage so we just took the biggest tier.  She said:  Bring it!

Home we went after hugging Katie and kissing her some more and got help and left with two more tiers.  Katie my angel covered the three tiers!!! She was my angel.  The mother of the bride broke in tears, I guess she was worried that I was never going to serve the wedding cake.  These were tears of relief!  We ended up having the most beautiful cake and thanks to Katie.  Katie also build the cake, putting all three tiers together.  Then explained to me how to decorate it.  She told me to get a photo of the container of her fondant because she had tried all the different brands and that was the best of them all.  She said that mine was impossible to work with since it was too dry!  Katie is a walking ANGEL.  I love her, love her and love her.  All the kisses and hugs are not enough to thank her for saving the wedding cake and so much more.'}]}

No taste to meat. Bread was possibly older than a day in the way it tasted. They were very nice but it was not as good as a Subway ot any chain.'}, {'review_stars': 3.0, 'review_date': '2022-01-07 02:57:32', 'review_text': 'This place is a small deli and they sell sandwiches and sushi. I ordered a tri-tip sandwich from here. The sandwich came with a half-foot French bread with red onions, tomatoes, lettuce, and tri-tip with mayo. It was mostly bread and there was barely anything in the sandwich. Overall, it tasted okay and the service is quick.'}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2021-09-15 20:50:43', 'review_text': "Just randomly saw this place on Yelp today and had to go try it as I'm Asian so I'm always on the lookout for new Asian places to try because I need my Asian food fix from time to time -- it's a special diet I have :P

I was a little skeptical based on their seemingly random array of food selections but I went for it anyways despite my husband saying he wasn't impressed with a sandwich he got there once.

To my delight, this place was totally awesome! It's a cute little deli in an industrial area so they get a lot of foot traffic during lunchtime from all the workers nearby. There's indoor and outdoor seating and the cute Asian couple that owns it are so nice and sweet. 

I got the lunch combo with chicken teriyaki and California roll -- it comes with miso soup, rice, salad and 2 egg rolls. The miso soup was authentic and delicious, the teriyaki chicken was sweet/savory and grilled to perfection with bits of charred ends, the egg rolls were hot and crispy, the California roll was fresh and moist, and the salad was tossed with a tasty sesame ginger dressing. In terms of flavor, it's honestly on par with a lot of real Japanese restaurant bento lunches. 

It was also a lot of food for under $15! I couldn't finish it all so I'll also have leftovers to eat for dinner tonight. I have yet to try the other menu items but I'll be sure to come back again soon!"}]}

And that, friends, is just the beginning of a dream come true.  

This sandwich might just be at the very tippy top of the hot, messy, cheesy, roast beef creations I've ever sampled.  It's difficult to describe the coming together of the meat, cheese, and very little else -- there's optional bell peppers for those who want them -- piled high on a thin, crunchy and unbeatably tasty torpedo roll.  

Nice people here, working very hard to manage a large and obviously seasoned (no pun intended) repeat crowd of locals.  And to think I'd have never found it without the urging of friends whose home is serving as a retreat from the desert heat.  

The challenge:  staying away for at least a few days before I go back for another dive into sandwich nirvana."}, {'review_stars': 4.0, 'review_date': '2021-06-03 19:26:23', 'review_text': 'My wife and always hope the Three Pickles sandwich shoppe is open when we fly into Santa Barbara Airport. {'name': "Cabo's Baja Grill & Cantina", 'address': '5096 Carpinteria Ave', 'city': 'Carpinteria', 'state': 'CA', 'categories': 'Restaurants, Mexican', 'hours': None, 'attributes': {'BusinessParking': {'garage': False, 'street': True, 'validated': False, 'lot': False, 'valet': False}, 'RestaurantsReservations': False, 'OutdoorSeating': True, 'WiFi': 'no', 'RestaurantsTakeOut': True, 'RestaurantsGoodForGroups': True, 'Music': None, 'Ambience': {'romantic': False, 'intimate': False, 'classy': False, 'hipster': False, 'divey': False, 'touristy': False, 'trendy': False, 'upscale': False, 'casual': True}}, 'business_stars': 3.0, 'review_info': [{'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2015-02-23 02:01:38', 'review_text': "Loving the new restaurant Cielo in Carpinteria! Looks amazing, food was perfect and great prices! Service was five star and the pricing was 2 star but tasted 5 star!!!!! I'll definitely be back :))"}, {'review_stars': 3.0, 'review_date': '2015-02-21 03:27:48', 'review_text': 'CBG is out of business.  It is now Cielo.  There is a full bar, and the menu is a mix of flatbreads, burgers, tex-mex, assorted appetizers.  There is inside and outside seating.  Bar has 8 spaces so get there early.  I did not have a cocktail so I cannot comment on the bartenders mixing skills. Except I did observe the pop rocks martini...hmmm childhood memories ;)
I had the steak flatbread with onions and cheese.  Crust was paper thin and the steak was good but I was disappointed by a lack of seasoning.  The steak flatbread was overpriced for what you get.  The trip tip plate (tri tip, beans, rice and tortillas ) for 8 dollars looked like a better deal.'}, {'review_stars': 2.0, 'review_date': '2015-02-20 22:15:34', 'review_text': 'Today's agenda:  1. Head to Carpinteria  2. Try out the new Cielo's restaurant  3. Get food poisoning

Trust me this was NOT the intention.

My wife and I were so excited when Cielo's finally opened it's doors to the public after weeks of renovations after the take over of Cabo's.  Cabos' was one of our favorite go to spots for lunch and were hoping that Cielo's captured us as a regular the way it's predecessor did.

When we arrived we did notice the positives. The interior was definitely upgraded as the booths give it a more restaurant ambiance.  The bathrooms were nicely done and very clean.

We sat in the front patio overlooking Carpinteria Blvd and took our first look at the new menus.  First thing we notice were the prices were about 25% higher than Cabos which is cool if the food warranted that price.  It's the standard beach diner fare with a bit of a higher end take on it.  Sliders, burgers, chicken plates, salads, apps, etc.   Were were disappointed at the price of the drinks (house margs were $10) and NO pitchers of margs anymore!  That sucked as that was one of the highlights of Cabos.   

First time there we ordered the sliders.  Not bad but not great.  Heavy jalapeno overrode the beefy flavor of the meat.  They had a nice dessert menu and we ordered the carrot cake.  Not bad but the server had no idea where it was sourced from.  

The 2nd time we came we ordered the 1/4 chicken plate as that was our favorite dish at Cabo's.  Nightmare thru and thru.  Our plates arrived and we just looked at each other as the rice was in clumps.  Literally.  The rice and beans were cold to the touch and when we cut into the chicken it was RAW.   Dark pink RAW.  We called over the server and she apologized and took it back.  The same plates were returned with the same pieces of chicken grilled to a near burnt crisp (chefs revenge I'm guessing) and the rice and beans were still cold.  We called over the server to let her know and one of the owners came over to apologize.   We told her that we REALLY want to like this place as we LOVED Cabo's but the standards of the restaurant need to start with the standards in the kitchen and there is just NO care for the food here.  Even the cold beans had no flavor.  I texted my brother that we were here and his reply was "nice patio...terrible food".  

Anyway unfortunately unless there are some major changes in the kitchen I'm never coming back to Cielo's.   Post script the only reason I gave it 2 stars instead of 1 was the server was VERY nice and very helpful'}]}

The third review had a negative experience with food poisoning from the 1/4 chicken plate, which was raw and the rice and beans were cold. The server was very nice and helpful, but the standards in the kitchen need improvement.

There were no sides and no variety of appetizers. After reading the reviews of the food I was sad to see the menu look so bare and lonely. 
Our server was great, very friendly and the Sommelier was excellent. I chose the mushrooms, the lobster pasta and chocolate mousse. The mushrooms were hearty but the lobster pasta only had about 5 small pieces of lobster. My husband ordered the Ceasar salad, salmon and crème brûlée. He enjoyed his salad but the salmon filet was small.  With 3 (possibly small) 
 items per person, you may want to grab an appetizer in the lobby bar before you come. 
It also took forever to get each course. We were seated at 6:55pm and didn't leave until 9:25pm. My husband and son went back to the room while I waited at the table an additional 15 minutes for 2 cookies and a side of vanilla ice cream. I asked a manager who walked by to check on the dessert and to get me a copy of the itemized receipt. He seemed annoyed. Overall, maybe I'll return when the regular menu is back.."}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2021-12-22 22:11:03', 'review_text': "First off, I want to say all of the staff were amazing but we loved our server Autumn. She was so sweet and really knew the menu. We ordered everything she recommended. 

We got a bottle of wine to start, very crisp and light. Then for appetizers we ordered a dozen oysters which came in fresh from Baja, CA. I love that they provided a large amount of lemon, hot sauce and this amazing house made sauce. I could have eaten 100. One of the best selection of oysters we had. The quail and side salad was phenomenal. Just watch out for the little bones.

For dinner, I got the halibut which was so rich and tender I could barely scoop it up. It was very flavorful. Bae got the filet and potatoes which was cooked to perfection. We got a side of the cauliflower and it was the perfect side. 

We opted for the chocolate mousse cake since chocolate is my favorite but to be honest wouldn't recommend. I wish they would expand their dessert menu but overall great night sitting by the water!"}, {'review_stars': 4.0, 'review_date': '2021-12-10 20:06:30', 'review_text': 'Had a great time! {'name': 'Pro Karaoke System Rentals', 'address': '', 'city': 'Santa Barbara', 'state': 'CA', 'categories': 'Party & Event Planning, Music Venues, Event Planning & Services, Nightlife, Arts & Entertainment', 'hours': None, 'attributes': {'BusinessParking': None, 'RestaurantsReservations': None, 'OutdoorSeating': None, 'WiFi': None, 'RestaurantsTakeOut': None, 'RestaurantsGoodForGroups': True, 'Music': None, 'Ambience': None}, 'business_stars': 5.0, 'review_info': [{'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2017-03-08 01:05:16', 'review_text': 'The Mike is very service-oriented, friendly and gave great advice. He made it all very simple, and for me that could be a challenge,  the speakers were set up to provide great sound.\nHe is a local guy, knows all the best stories and really works to make it simple for you, but also a great deal of fun.'}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2016-05-05 20:42:54', 'review_text': 'I celebrated my birthday with 15 friends recently, and rented a private event room in a local restaurant here in Santa Barbara. Mike was there right on time to set up the system and show me how to use it. When we were done for the night, we left the system there for him to pick up. Everything I needed was provided: a couple of mics, a few songbooks, and a signup sheet. The system itself was very easy to use, and I appreciated being able to adjust as needed, from the key of the song to the volume of both the music and the singer. It\'s nice not to have to shout to be heard over karaoke in a regular bar, and I loved being able to adjust the key even mid-song! The song selection was big and varied, and having the songbook available as a PDF was very helpful: several of us downloaded the PDF onto our phones/tablets, which made it easier to search for songs, both during and before the event. Fantastic! This was the line on loop in my head a few nights ago at Night Lizard. After several visits to this State Street brewery, I can easily say it's one of my tops. Staff are friendly, beers are tasty, and the vibe--whether sitting indoors or out--is neat. Gotta love all those hanging plants. 

Among beers tried are the Gnatcatcher IPA, Tiger Salamander Saison, and Monkeyflower Double White Ale. All are solid brews. I have yet to sample the food menu from Yona Redz, but look forward to it. 

There's usually room to sit on the outdoor patio, a Covid-safer option that offers great people-watching. I love holding a tulip glass of a tasty beverage, seeing folks passing by and eying the place. Thinking at them 'yes, you do want to stop here!' I smile a bit when someone does."}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2022-01-01 00:48:08', 'review_text': 'Trivia night is soooo cool. Best kombucha and seltzer. I want a restaurant to succeed here, but where is the branding? The name Sublime comes with no info as to what kind of food is served. There is no identity established and no web presence. Even if the sign said, in big letters, "Sublime: Healthy California Cuisine" it would help a lot. To the owner: this is very basic business 101. You can't open a restaurant in 2015 without a website and a name that hints to the consumer what type of food you are serving!'}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2015-06-25 14:18:40', 'review_text': 'Awesome coffee! Nice cafe in the area, looks like the menu is changing since they opened. Good to have a place to grab a beer and dinner on Milpas.'}, {'review_stars': 2.0, 'review_date': '2015-06-24 21:54:19', 'review_text': "Sublime is new, okay we get it, but methinks a place should have their ish together just a smidge before opening their doors.  

Ambiance - sterile.  

Service - servers were nice, but slow and clueless.  They could not answer a single question about anything without having to run to the back to ask someone else.  

Food - decent enough.  I enjoyed the sage chicken.  My friend had the $40 steak dish (which I see is no longer on the menu) and the steak was juicy and delicious.  Congrats!  You made a yummy!  However, they use Miracle Whip in their deviled eggs.  Rewind....whuuuut??  Ew and moving on.  I looked forward to trying their tiramisu and after the server hyped it up, he told us it was not ready for the eating and wouldn't be until the next day.  Tiramisu tease!

A place that charges $40 for a dish should NEVER have Miracle Whip on the premises!!!  Where is your pride?!  Ohhh yeah, it's at the bottom of that jar of Miracle Whip.  It's miracle if they stay open, and it certainly won't be attributed to my returning patronage."}]}

{'name': 'Elements Restaurant and Bar', 'address': '129 E Anapamu St', 'city': 'Santa Barbara', 'state': 'CA', 'categories': 'Bars, Restaurants, American (New), Nightlife', 'hours': {'Monday': '11:30-21:0', 'Tuesday': '11:30-21:0', 'Wednesday': '11:30-21:0', 'Friday': '11:30-22:0', 'Saturday': '11:30-22:0', 'Sunday': '10:0-21:0'}, 'attributes': {'BusinessParking': {'garage': False, 'street': True, 'validated': False, 'lot': False, 'valet': False}, 'RestaurantsReservations': True, 'OutdoorSeating': True, 'WiFi': None, 'RestaurantsTakeOut': False, 'RestaurantsGoodForGroups': True, 'Music': None, 'Ambience': {'romantic': False, 'intimate': False, 'touristy': False, 'hipster': False, 'divey': False, 'classy': False, 'trendy': False, 'upscale': False, 'casual': False}}, 'business_stars': 3.5, 'review_info': [{'review_stars': 2.0, 'review_date': '2012-01-31 06:56:26', 'review_text': 'Is this place run by high school kids?

Took the time to make a reservation for New Year's Eve a month in advance as this restaurant has had special significance for my girlfriend and I.  Well dressed and looking forward to the prix fixe menu we walked in at 9pm to a half empty restaurant only to be sat on the patio.  Upset that after making the reservation so far in advance we were sat on the chilly patio.  I protested to the hostess only to be told that the empty tables were "reserved."  This is where I became enough of a pain in the ass to get the table we reserved.  It is worth noting that the restaurant sat half-empty for the entirety of our meal.

After being reseated inside we began the prix fixe dinner only to have food, obviously produced en masse and lacking attention to quality, thrown at us with such rapidity and voracity that we were almost unable to taste its run of the mill composition.  Almost.

Add to all of this an obnoxiously loud bartender and a waitstaff more interested in taking numerous cigarette breaks, and I'm very lucky to say that we managed to have an amazing and memorable evening DESPITE our experience at Elements.

It is also worth noting that this restaurant is up for sale and I surmise that the current owner, possibly already in retirement and looking for someone to reinvigorate Elements, has handed over the reins to his inept staff.  This once wonderful restaurant seems to have gone the way of the dogs.'}, {'review_stars': 2.0, 'review_date': '2012-01-30 17:24:10', 'review_text': "Came to Elements with a couple of friends for Brunch on a Sunday morning, mostly for the bottomless mimosa. There were 5 adults and 1 child, but only 4 adults ordered food. The food was average- nothing amazing. Once we got the bill we noticed that they had already included a 20% gratuity to our bill, which was fine. But once we got home and compared our receipts/ bank accounts we realized that they added an ADDITIONAL gratuity once we left! Very sneaky. Only thing I'll be returning for is a price adjustment."}, {'review_stars': 4.0, 'review_date': '2012-01-17 02:33:53', 'review_text': "This is really a 4 1/2 star rating...

ambiance: cozy, quiet, beautiful view of Sunken Gardens
service: 5 stars- attentive, helpful, friendly, prompt
Drinks: 
wine by the glass: lacking in depth.  Didn't have any cocktails but the list looked great.  Need to come back and order the Firetini that seems so popular.

Food: fresh, crispy but soft bread with creamy butter brought to the table.
Appetizer: ceviche with tortilla chips- thick, super-crunchy chips, ceviche light and flavorful
My entree: panko-crusted swordfish with saffron basmati rice, red and green peppers, and thai coconut sauce- delicious, not heavy, lightly breaded and perfectly cooked.
Hubby's entree: duck breast and sausage with lentils which was rich and very flavorful.
Side: truffle fries with ketchup- yum!  very generous portion.

overall: 4 1/2 stars, will certainly be returning!"}]}

{'name': "Peabody's", 'address': '1198 Coast Village Rd', 'city': 'Santa Barbara', 'state': 'CA', 'categories': 'Bars, Nightlife, American (Traditional), Restaurants', 'hours': None, 'attributes': {'BusinessParking': {'garage': False, 'street': True, 'validated': False, 'lot': False, 'valet': False}, 'RestaurantsReservations': True, 'OutdoorSeating': True, 'WiFi': None, 'RestaurantsTakeOut': True, 'RestaurantsGoodForGroups': True, 'Music': None, 'Ambience': {'romantic': False, 'intimate': False, 'touristy': False, 'hipster': False, 'divey': False, 'classy': False, 'trendy': False, 'upscale': False, 'casual': False}}, 'business_stars': 3.5, 'review_info': [{'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2012-05-10 17:48:11', 'review_text': 'The last time I went to Peabody's I had such a good time I just had to write a review.  Here's what happened:  I was sitting at the bar drinking a beer and eating some nachos when this gorgeous girl, Katie, and her friend walked in. They sat next to me and I noticed that Katie had the same phone as me, a crappy freebie phone they give you when you lose your iphone, and so I said, "Hey I like your crappy cell-phone, did you lose your iphone like me?"  As it turned out she had.  We swapped stories about how we had drunkenly lost our phones and after that we just kept talking.  She was making me laugh telling raunchy jokes and I was trying to impress her by weaving beach volleyball stories into the conversation.

The whole time the bartender kept our drinks flowing and made sure we were having a good time.  At one point when the conversation ran dry and I was about to pay my bill and go. Luckily I got the signal from the bartender, "stick around, this girl likes you." I'm hopelessly ignorant to the subtle signals of the opposite sex, and if I hadn't taken his advice I probably would have blown it.  We kept talking for a while and I finally asked her for her number.  Not only did she give me her number, but she walked with me to my car, gave me a kiss on the cheek, flashed me a smile, and made sure I was going to call her.

Needless to say, I did.  We met up a few days later for drinks and we had a great time.  We've been hanging out pretty steadily for a few weeks now and things are going really well.  I had to write a review and thank the guys at Peabody's for hooking me up.  I'm a regular at Peabody's now for sure.'}, {'review_stars': 4.0, 'review_date': '2012-03-09 09:42:31', 'review_text': "My girlfriend and I were staying at the hotel behind this restaurant and decided to try it out after a long day of shopping and sight seeing. After reading some previous comments about service, we were a bit hesitant but decided to roll the dice. We were pleased to find that this place stayed open till midnight on weeknights. We arrived on a Thursday @ 10:30 p.m and were quickly greeted and told to sit anywhere we wanted. We sat next to a warm fire at the fireplace in the lounge but waited a very very long time for someone to take our order........we immediately started talking about the poor service stories we read earlier. After waiting for an eternity, I was forced to walk our order to the bartender (Warren) personally which he promptly took care of. We received our food and drinks in a timely manner and found the calamari and sliders to be hot , fresh, and very tasty. Again, we found ourselves searching for a server to refill our beers but to no avail and ended up walking to the bar for service. Although we found the service to be substandard as we read earlier.....the bartender (Warren) eventually personally came to our table and apologized for us being neglected and without hesitation offered to buy us a round of beers and shots of tequila. With that said, the service was as expected (we did get there @ 10:30 p.m. though) the food was very good, and Warren going above and beyond any other bartender ever has for us made our evening a very enjoyable experience. We highly recommend this restaurant and ask for Warren - he'll take great care of you."}, {'review_stars': 2.0, 'review_date': '2012-01-31 02:06:09', 'review_text': 'ugh.... this place is horrible.
          I have been here many, many times I can honesly cant remember a plate that I ordered that was any good.  Whatever you do, do NOT order any pasta.  Its rubbery and microwaved every single time....
      This is a real bummer because my wife and I went on our first date here and to be totally frank, I still have no desire to ever return.
      This place is totally avoidable....'}]}

{'name': 'Choppa Poke', 'address': '7000 Hollister Ave, Ste 102', 'city': 'Goleta', 'state': 'CA', 'categories': 'Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Food, Poke, Hawaiian, Seafood', 'hours': {'Monday': '11:0-21:0', 'Tuesday': '11:0-21:0', 'Wednesday': '11:0-21:0', 'Thursday': '11:0-21:0', 'Friday': '11:0-22:0', 'Saturday': '11:0-15:0', 'Sunday': '11:0-22:0'}, 'attributes': {'BusinessParking': {'garage': False, 'street': False, 'validated': False, 'lot': True, 'valet': False}, 'RestaurantsReservations': False, 'OutdoorSeating': None, 'WiFi': 'free', 'RestaurantsTakeOut': True, 'RestaurantsGoodForGroups': True, 'Music': None, 'Ambience': {'touristy': False, 'hipster': False, 'romantic': False, 'divey': False, 'intimate': False, 'trendy': True, 'upscale': False, 'classy': False, 'casual': True}}, 'business_stars': 4.0, 'review_info': [{'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2022-01-11 08:52:50', 'review_text': 'Choppa Poke is in a plaza with a big parking lot. I came here on a Monday night and there was no wait time. 

I ordered a medium bowl which was VERY filling! They are extremely generous with the portion size and especially the protein scoops (which most poke places tend to skimp out on). The fish and all the toppings were fresh and overall it was really delicious. 

Choppa is more expensive when compared to other poke places; however, the price is justified by the fresh fish and generous portions! Will definitely come again(:'}, {'review_stars': 1.0, 'review_date': '2022-01-01 05:56:37', 'review_text': 'WORST experience ever. If you wish to be humiliated, do check them out.
 
Ordered their poke bowl in english and dined in with my husband. Thinking we could not understand mandarin, we later heard one of the female employees speaking to her colleagues pretty loudly about us and mentioning how disgusting we were as customers. In her exact words "Those two people who just came in, one male and female, having a bowl of poke bowl. Don't you think they are very disgusting?"  

I was extremely astonished to hear what she had said given that we did absolutely no wrong. And still find it puzzling why she would have said that. While we did not confront about the issue, we later walked over and spoke in mandarin, to let her know we understood mandarin too.  I guess it's just having a certain amount of basic respect and EQ to not badmouth about your customers so loudly. I hope the boss of this outlet is reading this and would educate your staff about customer service.  

I've never felt so much humiliation while dining.'}, {'review_stars': 4.0, 'review_date': '2021-12-18 02:17:03', 'review_text': "Located next to Kyle's Kitchen across from the Costco Center, this place offers up delicious poke bowls with all the fixings. I get the medium, which comes with 3 scoops of protein. For me, that means 2 scoops of fresh ahi and 1 of spicy tuna. Like all poke bowl spots, be careful if you order any mayo sauce options as you will quickly be asking for poke with your mayo. I like some ponzu sauce and a touch of wasabi mayo to go along with almost all the toppings + extra ginger. It's always fast and mostly polite service. 

I do feel compelled to dock a star for the prices. A medium will run you $20 with tax. Considering it's delicious and healthy, I'll splurge every now and again, but I'm just not sure how long it can survive. So far so good and I'm glad it's there. Definitely worth checking out."}]}

The place is beautiful, but some issues with the bill and somewhat the food.

I had both the Crispy Yukon grilled potatoes, and the Lido's breakfast sandwich. The potatoes were delicious, and I found them to be more delicious than the sandwich. The potatoes also had a very generous serving.

The main issue I have, is the 20% service fee that is automatically added to your bill. It's not stated anywhere on the menu, or by the servers. Now I don't mind paying a 20% service fee if I was aware of it, but this restaurant does not notify you about it. Do not be fooled. Add tip on top of this, and you have a fee that's almost 40-50% of your meal. Would not come again."}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2021-09-10 01:53:26', 'review_text': "New hours and the system as of Sept 5...now the restaurant is open for breakfast from 7am instead of 8.  Also, you can place your order with a server if you're dining in (instead of lining up at the bar counter).

The catch is...if you would like to dine outdoors, you'd have to wait till 8am when the pool opens.  Also, be aware it gets very busy between 9am and 10:30sh weekends as usual.

All servers are consistently good, friendly, and efficient!

Their lunch and dinner options are good as well.  I was going to have dinner @ Costa, but ended up eating (mussels) here after seeing someone else's meal.  ;)"}, {'review_stars': 2.0, 'review_date': '2021-08-18 17:19:00', 'review_text': "The food is OK. The granola was not bad. The date shake was so so as well. It really got to me was it took forever for the waiter to come to the table. 

I'm top of that I can't stand companies that demand 20% gratuity regardless of service. I understand if it's a table of six or more etc. or even demanding 15 or 18 percent. But as someone who typically tips over 25% when I am demanded it really bothers me. If this has to do with fair wages then raise the prices of the items. Then you leave it up to the market to decide. But there's nothing posted anywhere that says a mandatory 20% tip. That's my other problem. If you're going to require that we need to disclose it on the front door."}]}

I point to the plates on our table to show her we didn't. I ask for our money back and she declines. We end up getting into a fight about one chicken taco and guac.
